text:
You are building an admin product catalog page for an e-commerce team.

The catalog is read-heavy and should feel fast for day-to-day browsing, so avoid re-querying product data on every request.

Admins can trigger a "Sync latest catalog" action from the page when upstream ERP/PIM data changes (pricing, inventory, availability). After submitting, they should be able to continue working immediately, even if the product list is briefly stale.

The expected behavior is that product data is refreshed in the background and becomes up to date shortly after, across any views that depend on the same catalog data.

Implement the sync trigger as a regular HTML form that uses an inline Server Action in the page.
Use the cache tag name "products" consistently for catalog caching and invalidation.

In practice, sync jobs can touch thousands of SKUs, so operators prioritize a responsive admin experience and eventual consistency across catalog views over forcing every request to block on freshly recomputed data.

I’m going to inspect the app structure to find the catalog page, the current data fetching path, and where caching/invalidation is wired so I can switch the sync action to an inline Server Action with `products` tag-based refresh.

text: I found a very small Next app, so I’m reading the page, layout, and any DB helpers next to locate the product query and the sync entry point. Then I’ll wire the form/action and refresh the shared `products` cache tag rather than forcing blocking recomputation.

I’ve confirmed the app is basically a stub, so I’m checking the current Next cache API surface to implement this the right way for the canary version here. After that I’ll replace the placeholder page with the catalog UI and a form-backed server action.

I have the implementation shape: a cached catalog snapshot tagged `products`, plus an inline server action that simulates the ERP/PIM sync and then invalidates that same tag with stale-while-revalidate semantics. I’m patching the data helper, page, and metadata now.
The page and cache helper are patched. I’m running a build now to catch any Next 16 canary syntax issues around `use cache`, `cacheTag`, or the inline server action before I hand it back.

text: Build is still running; I’m polling for the result and will fix anything surfaced rather than leaving the app in a half-checked state.

The build exposed two concrete issues: `use cache` needs `cacheComponents` enabled, and the page can’t use `styled-jsx` from a Server Component. I’m switching the styles to a global stylesheet and turning on the cache feature flag so the `products` tag-based caching compiles cleanly.
I’ve moved styling out of the Server Component and enabled the cache components flag. I’m rebuilding now to verify the canary-specific cache APIs and the server action behavior end to end.

text: Final build is in progress with the cleaned config. I’m checking the result and then I’ll summarize the exact behavior change and the files touched.

Implemented the admin catalog page with a cached product snapshot and an inline Server Action sync form.

What changed:
- `app/page.tsx`
- Added a read-heavy catalog UI.
- Added a regular HTML `<form>` with an inline Server Action.
- The action simulates syncing upstream catalog data, then calls `revalidateTag('products', 'max')` so the current request stays responsive and dependent views refresh in the background.
- The page reads from a cached helper that uses the `products` tag consistently.

- `lib/db.js`
- Added `getCatalogSnapshot()` with `use cache` and `cacheTag('products')`.
- Added `syncCatalogSource()` to mutate the simulated upstream catalog data.

- `app/globals.css`
- Moved styling into a global stylesheet so the page stays a Server Component-compatible route.

- `app/layout.tsx`
- Updated metadata to match the catalog admin app and imported global styles.

- `next.config.ts`
- Enabled `cacheComponents` so `use cache` works in this Next canary version.

Verification:
- `npm run build` passes.

Behavior:
- The product list is cached by the `products` tag.
- Clicking “Sync latest catalog” submits a normal form, returns immediately, and revalidates the shared `products` cache in the background.
- Any view using the same `products` cache tag can pick up the refreshed data shortly after, without forcing every request to block.

If you want, I can also add:
1. A success banner using `useFormStatus`/`useActionState`
2. A separate shared catalog data module for multiple routes
3. A mocked “last sync job” status panel for operators
